Function [CC]: FUNCTION: N-methyltransferase participating in the biosynthesis of natural products derived from phenylethylamine, including mescaline, a natural hallucinogen potentially used in psychotherapeutic treatments (PubMed:37675639). Catalyzes the N-methylation of many substrates, including 3-methoxytyramine, 5-hydroxy-3,4-dimethoxyphenethylamine, 4-hydroxy-3,5-dimethoxyphenethylamine, tyramine and mescaline (PubMed:37675639). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:37675639}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: N-methyltransferase participating in the biosynthesis of natural products derived from phenylethylamine, including mescaline, a natural hallucinogen potentially used in psychotherapeutic treatments (PubMed:37675639, PubMed:38835170). Catalyzes the N-methylation of many substrates, including tyramine, N-methyltyramine, dopamine, N-methyl-dopamine, 3-methoxytyramine, N-methyl-3-methoxytyramine, 3,4-dihydroxy-5-methoxyphenethylamine, N-methyl-3,4-dihydroxy-5-methoxyphenethylamine, 3-hydroxy-4,5-dimethoxyphenethylamine, N-methyl-3-hydroxy-4,5-dimethoxyphenethylamine, 4-hydroxy-3,5-dimethoxyphenethylamine, N-methyl-4-hydroxy-3,5-dimethoxyphenethylamine, mescaline and N-methylmescaline (PubMed:37675639, PubMed:38835170). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:37675639, ECO:0000269|PubMed:38835170}.
